plan.failureSummary: The test failed because the URL returned by the search result now contains a fragment identifier (#playwright-check-suite), whereas the test expects an exact match with the base URL.
observedIndicators: Assertion mismatch: Received URL contains #playwright-check-suite fragment while Expected does not.
page.ai successfully completed its objective but the following deterministic assertion failed.
History shows a streak of 7 failures, suggesting a persistent change in the application.

remediationSteps.summary: Relax or update the URL assertion to account for URL fragments.
remediationSteps.details: Update the assertion at line 13 of checkly.test.ts to either include the fragment '#playwright-check-suite' or use a more flexible matcher like '.toContain()' or a regex to allow for anchors.

remediationSteps.summary: Normalize URL comparison in the test script.
remediationSteps.details: If the specific anchor is variable, consider using 'new URL(page.url()).pathname' to compare only the path.

plan.notes: The page.ai instruction to 'choose the match for Constructs > DETECT' was successfully executed by the agent. The agent clicked a link whose href was '/docs/constructs/playwright-check#playwright-check-suite'. The subsequent hardcoded Playwright assertion 'expect(page.url()).toEqual(...)' failed because it did not account for the hash. This is a clear case of assertion drift where the application's linking behavior has become more specific.

originalPlaywrightArgs: Checkly dev docs search|Fill out a survey
